# Microsoft eShopOnWeb ASP.NET Core Reference Application

> eShop sample applications have been updated and moved to https://github.com/dotnet/eShop. Active development will continue there. We also recommend the [Reliable Web App](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/web-apps/guides/reliable-web-app/overview) patterns guidance for building web apps with enterprise app patterns.


> A new community supported version of eShopOnWeb can be found at https://github.com/NimblePros/eShopOnWeb

After logging in with the following command, you will be able to use the azd cli to quickly provision and deploy the application.

```
azd auth login
```

Then, execute the `azd init` command to initialize the environment.
```
azd init -t dotnet-architecture/eShopOnWeb 
```

Run `azd up` to provision all the resources to Azure and deploy the code to those resources.
```
azd up 
```

According to the prompt, enter an `env name`, and select `subscription` and `location`, these are the necessary pa

dotnet ef migrations add InitialModel --context catalogcontext -p ../Infrastructure/Infrastructure.csproj -s Web.csproj -o Data/Migrations

    dotnet ef migrations add InitialIdentityModel --context appidentitydbcontext -p ../Infrastructure/Infrastructure.csproj -s Web.csproj -o Identity/Migrations
    ```

## Running the sample in the dev container

This project includes a `.devcontainer` folder with a [dev container configuration](https://containers.dev/), which lets you use a container a

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wbreza/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json

name: eShopOnWeb
services:
  web:
    project: ./src/Web
    language: csharp
    host: appservice

version: '3.4'
services:
 eshopwebmvc:
   environment:
     - ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Docker
     - ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:8080
   ports:
     - "5106:8080"
   volumes:
     - ~/.aspnet/https:/root/.aspnet/https:ro
     - ~/.microsoft/usersecrets:/root/.microsoft/usersecrets:ro
 eshoppublicapi:
   environment:
     - ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Docker
     - ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:8080
   ports:
     - "5200:8080"
   volumes:
     - ~/.aspnet/https:/root/.aspnet/https:ro
     - ~/.microsoft/userse

version: '3.4'

services:
  eshopwebmvc:
    image: ${DOCKER_REGISTRY-}eshopwebmvc
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: src/Web/Dockerfile
    depends_on:
      - "sqlserver"
  eshoppublicapi:
    image: ${DOCKER_REGISTRY-}eshoppublicapi
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: src/PublicApi/Dockerfile
    depends_on:
      - "sqlserver"
  sqlserver:
    image: mcr.microsoft.com/azure-sql-edge
    ports:
      - "1433:1433"
    environment:
      - SA_PASSWORD=@someThingComplicated

# Dev container

This project includes a [dev container](https://containers.dev/), which lets you use a container as a full-featured dev environment.

You can use the dev container configuration in this folder to build and run the app without needing to install any of its tools locally! You can use it in [GitHub Codespaces](https://github.com/features/codespaces) or the [VS Code Dev Containers extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers).

##

    - name: Test with dotnet
      run: dotnet test ./eShopOnWeb.sln --configuration Release

# Access Control Matrix — eShopOnWeb

> Who can do what, and — critically — WHERE that boundary is actually enforced (database? application code? nowhere?). Every cell below is grounded in a specific authorization attribute, guard clause, or live-database absence-of-mechanism finding.

---

## Roles That Exist

| Role | Source of definition | Live evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **Anonymous / Guest** | Implicit — anyone without a valid auth cookie | N/A |
| **Authenticated Customer** | Any row in `As

# Conceptual Data Model — eShopOnWeb

> Strips away table names, column types, and foreign keys — and asks instead: "If you described this business to someone on a whiteboard, what are the THINGS it deals with, and how do they relate?" Written in business language for a non-technical stakeholder (e.g. a product owner at Gate G1) who needs to validate "is this what we think our business does?" before any migration plan is approved.

---

## The Core Concepts (the "nouns" of the business)

| Busin

# Data Dictionary — eShopOnWeb

> Every table and column listed in `schema-catalogue.json`, with its **business meaning** (not its technical type — that's already documented elsewhere). Where the codebase explains a field outright, that explanation is cited. Where it doesn't, the meaning below is honestly marked as *inferred from usage* — never invented from the column name alone. Entries that hide a real surprise get a full "Technical reality" breakdown; routine columns get a one-line entry so

### BuyerId
- **Business meaning**: Who this basket belongs to.
- **Technical reality**: A plain `character varying(256)` string holding the owner's username (== email — see `AspNetUsers.UserName` below) OR a temporary anonymous-session identifier. NO foreign key exists or could exist (cross-database). This is THE soft reference flagged throughout this analysis.
- **Source of definition**: Inferred from `BasketService` code path tracing. See `redundancy-analysis.json` CE-001, `erd.md` WARNING s

### UnitPrice
- **Business meaning**: The exact price-per-unit the customer agreed to pay for this line item, frozen at order creation.
- **Technical reality**: Copied from `BasketItem.UnitPrice` at checkout (`OrderService.cs:45`) — i.e., it inherits BR-001's "first price wins" lock from the basket stage, then freezes again permanently. The live `Catalog.Price` is consulted ONLY to refresh the product name/picture for the snapshot, never to re-price this field.
- **Source of definition**: Code-c

- **Confidence**: 1.0

## Table: `__EFMigrationsHistory` (eShopIdentity)
- **Business meaning**: None — EF Core's internal migration-tracking table (same purpose as its `eShopCatalog` counterpart, in a completely separate physical database).
- **Live content**: 1 row — `20260526102952_InitialPostgres` / EF 8.0.4. **Confidence**: 1.0

---

# Cross-Cutting: Concepts Defined in Code But Backed By NO Table At All

### `Buyer.IdentityGuid`, `PaymentMethod.CardId` / `Last4` / `Alias`
- **Business mean
```

# Data Quality Report — eShopOnWeb

> Every finding below is confirmed against the LIVE PostgreSQL 18.3 database at localhost:5432 — not inferred from code. Confidence: 1.0 unless noted.

---

## DQ-001 — CRITICAL: Every single product picture is an unresolved placeholder URL

**Live evidence**: `SELECT "PictureUri", count(*) FROM "Catalog" GROUP BY "PictureUri"` returns **12 distinct rows, all 12 in the form**:
```
http://catalogbaseurltobereplaced/images/products/{1..12}.png
```
**Root cause**

**Live evidence**:
- `ApplicationCore/Constants/AuthorizationConstants.cs:11` — `JWT_SECRET_KEY = "SecretKeyOfDoomThatMustBeAMinimumNumberOfBytes"`, used to SIGN authentication tokens (`Infrastructure/Identity/IdentityTokenClaimService.cs:26`, `PublicApi/Program.cs:54`)
- **DUPLICATED across at least TWO separate project config files** — `Web/appsettings.json` AND `PublicApi/appsettings.json` both contain the IDENTICAL literal `"Password=Clarium123"` in both `CatalogConnection` and `IdentityConn

# DA Agent 2 — Review Summary

> Reviewed all 13 output files produced by DA Agent 1 against: (1) the unit/integration test suite, (2) `README.md`, (3) a fresh live-database re-verification, and (4) a targeted spot-check of source files Agent 1's draft had not opened. Then ran the mandatory 10-point cross-file consistency sweep and fixed every gap found. **Bottom line: Agent 1's findings held up well — zero findings were CORRECTED (i.e., zero were factually wrong); the review's value was in rais

- **Most important findings**:
  1. `BasketAddItem.KeepsOriginalUnitPriceIfMoreItemsAdded` — proves BR-001's price-lock is intentional and regression-tested (raised confidence 0.85→0.97)
  2. `TransferBasket.TransferAnonymousBasketItemsWhilePreservingExistingUserBasketItems` — proves BR-003's merge logic in granular detail, including that it correctly composes BR-001's price-lock into the merge
  3. `OrderTotal.*` — surfaced an entirely new finding (BR-007: totals are computed, never stored)
  4

risk: lower urgency, but left unresolved it will keep confusing every new developer
     who reads the domain model and assumes saved payment methods are a working feature.

    specifically to assert and protect this exact behaviour (confidence 0.97)

﻿# API Endpoints

This folder demonstrates how to configure API endpoints as individual classes. You can compare it to the traditional controller-based approach found in /Web/Controllers/Api.

# RUN ALL CONTAINERS FROM ROOT (folder with .sln file):
# docker-compose build
# docker-compose up
#
# RUN JUST THIS CONTAINER FROM ROOT (folder with .sln file):
# docker build --pull -t web -f src/Web/Dockerfile .
#
# RUN COMMAND
#  docker run --name eshopweb --rm -it -p 5106:5106 web
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 AS build
WORKDIR /app

COPY *.sln .
COPY . .
WORKDIR /app/src/Web
RUN dotnet restore

RUN dotnet publish -c Release -o out

FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0 AS runtime
